name: FastAPI
what_it_does: Modern, high-performance Python web framework for building APIs with automatic OpenAPI/JSON Schema generation, based on Python type hints and Pydantic. Provides async-first design with Starlette underneath, automatic interactive docs (Swagger UI + ReDoc), request validation, and serialization. Agents use FastAPI to build the APIs they expose or consume.
use_cases: Building REST APIs that agents expose to orchestrators or other agents
Creating well-typed API endpoints with automatic OpenAPI spec generation for agent consumption
Rapid prototyping of API backends with built-in validation and documentation
Serving ML models and AI inference endpoints with async request handling
Building agent tool servers where FastAPI auto-generates the OpenAPI spec tools can consume
not_for: Server-side rendered web applications (use Django or Flask with templates)
Very simple scripts or single-endpoint tools where overhead is unnecessary
Teams that don't use Python (use Express, Gin, or Spring Boot for other languages)
Agents that need to consume an external API — FastAPI is for building APIs, not consuming them
best_when: You are building a Python API backend that agents will consume, and you want automatic OpenAPI spec generation, type safety, and async performance with minimal boilerplate.
avoid_when: You need full-stack web framework features (ORM, admin UI, auth middleware) out of the box — Django provides those. For purely synchronous simple APIs, Flask has less overhead in terms of learning curve.

auth.notes: FastAPI has built-in security utilities via fastapi.security: OAuth2PasswordBearer, HTTPBearer, APIKeyHeader, HTTPBasic. These integrate with the Depends injection system. OAuth2 with scopes is first-class. JWT validation is not built-in but trivially added with python-jose or PyJWT. No auth is enforced by default — developers must add it explicitly.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free and open source (MIT). No usage limits.
pricing.paid_tiers: None. FastAPI itself has no paid tier.
pricing.notes: FastAPI is MIT-licensed open source. Deploy on any hosting provider. No vendor lock-in. Creator (Sebastián Ramírez) offers Tiangolo consulting, but the framework itself has no commercial restrictions.

performance.rate_limits: No built-in rate limiting. Add slowapi (based on limits) for rate limiting. Performance benchmarks show FastAPI comparable to NodeJS Express and significantly faster than Flask/Django for async workloads.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: FastAPI returns structured 422 Unprocessable Entity with detailed validation error arrays showing exactly which field failed and why (using Pydantic error messages). 422 errors include loc (field path), msg (human message), and type (error type). This is one of the best validation error formats for agent consumption — agents can parse the errors array and auto-correct requests.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: FastAPI is a framework, not a service — idempotency is the responsibility of the application developer. The framework provides no built-in idempotency key support. Developers must implement idempotency at the endpoint level.
known_agent_gotchas: FastAPI is a framework for building APIs, not an API to consume — agents build with it, not call it
Automatic OpenAPI spec at /openapi.json enables agents to discover and call FastAPI-built APIs dynamically
Pydantic v2 (FastAPI 0.100+) has breaking changes vs Pydantic v1 — check model syntax when reading examples
Background tasks run after the response is sent — agents cannot assume background work is complete when response arrives
Default error handler returns 422 for validation errors, 500 for unhandled exceptions — add custom exception handlers for agent-friendly error responses
CORS must be explicitly configured with CORSMiddleware — not enabled by default
Async endpoints require async database drivers — mixing sync and async can cause event loop blocking

security_score_components.security_notes: FastAPI provides OAuth2 and API key security utilities but does not enforce them — developers must opt in. No TLS at the framework level (handled by ASGI server like uvicorn + reverse proxy). Pydantic validation prevents injection attacks on typed fields. Security depends heavily on developer implementation. fastapi-users library adds production-ready auth with JWT, OAuth2, and session management.
